title: SeptCrawler
description: A custom search engine architecture currently being designed, focused on building a scalable pipeline for web crawling, indexing, and retrieval.
purpose: To design a multi-threaded crawling system in C++, exploring asynchronous I/O patterns with Node.js for request handling, and structuring efficient indexing and retrieval mechanisms.
learningOutcome: To deeply understand how search engines work internally by building core components from scratch, instead of relying on existing abstractions.

title: WorkshopHub
description: A full-stack workshop management platform with role-based access control for handling the lifecycle of educational events, from registrations to certificate issuance.
purpose: To build a centralised platform that reduces the administrative overhead of organising workshops, providing a seamless experience for both organisers and participants.
learningOutcome: Solid understanding of full-stack web development with the MERN stack, implementing secure JWT authentication and bcrypt-based password hashing, managing complex MongoDB relationships, and designing responsive UI with React.js and Tailwind CSS.

title: Priorify
description: A productivity-focused task manager that utilises priority-based ordering to help users focus on what truly matters. Features a clean console interface with a planned transition to a GUI.
purpose: To develop a lightweight, distraction-free tool for personal task management that prioritises efficiency and ease of use.
learningOutcome: Hands-on experience with C++ STL containers, persistent storage using SQLite, and designing user-centric CLI workflows.

title: URL Shortener
description: A simple URL shortening service that generates compact aliases for long URLs.
purpose: To understand how URL shorteners work by building a core backend service using Node.js, Express, MongoDB and Mongoose.
learningOutcome: Worked with Express routing and middleware, and implemented basic data storage and retrieval using MongoDB.

title: MyDayOff
description: An HR automation tool that simulates real-world leave and reimbursement approval workflows, featuring hierarchical roles and real-time status updates.
purpose: To build a practical application of role-based access control in a multi-user system with approval workflows.
learningOutcome: Gained experience in designing multi-user workflows and implementing role-based access control using the MERN stack.

title: FinTracker
description: A visual personal finance dashboard that provides insights into spending habits through charts and categorised transaction tracking.
purpose: To create a tool for better financial awareness using data visualization techniques.
learningOutcome: Explored data visualization with Recharts and real-time database integration with Firebase.

title: Karaoke App
description: A CLI-based karaoke app that fetches song lyrics using the Genius API, downloads audio using yt-dlp, and displays color-highlighted lyrics in sync with the music.
purpose: To build a Python application that combines lyric fetching and audio playback using the Genius API and external tools.
learningOutcome: Gained experience working with APIs (Genius API), handling external tools like yt-dlp and FFmpeg, and managing audio playback in Python.

title: Survivors Clone
description: A 2D top-down action game in Godot inspired by the 'bullet heaven' genre, featuring player movement, basic enemy AI, and level-up mechanics.
purpose: To learn the fundamentals of 2D game development, including player control, collision detection, and simple AI behaviors.
learningOutcome: Gaining experience with GDScript, Godot's scene tree, and implementing basic game systems like player upgrades and enemy spawning.

title: Coin Collector
description: A 3D micro-game focused on smooth player movement and item collection mechanics, built to explore the Unity Editor and C# scripting.
purpose: An introductory project to get familiar with the 3D development workflow and Unity's component-based architecture.
learningOutcome: Learned about 3D physics and using C# scripts to handle game logic and UI updates in Unity.

title: Snake Game
description: The classic Snake game, currently being built from scratch in C++ to practice low-level programming and game logic.
purpose: To practice implementing game mechanics and logic in C++ without using any game engine or framework.
learningOutcome: Gaining hands-on experience with C++ programming, handling user input, implementing game loops, and basic grid-based movement algorithms.

title: File Deletion Tool
description: A utility tool that provides a simple web interface for managing file deletion on a server, serving as a practical exercise in backend file system operations.
purpose: To understand Node.js core modules like 'fs' and safely handle file operations via a web interface.
learningOutcome: Gained hands-on experience with file handling in Node.js, performing basic server-side validation, and serving static files with Express.

title: Password Generator
description: A secure, client-side password generator that allows users to customize length and character sets for strong password creation.
purpose: To build a practical tool while practicing DOM manipulation and string operations in pure JavaScript.
learningOutcome: Practised creating accessible UIs, handling clipboard interactions, and implementing randomization logic in the browser.

title: Roll the Dice
description: An interactive dice-rolling application that supports concurrent rolling of multiple dice with dynamic visual updates.
purpose: A small project to practice JavaScript logic and responsive CSS design.
learningOutcome: Gained experience implementing interactive UI elements, handling randomization in JavaScript, and adding visual effects with CSS.
